Blavatsky cites Origen extensively in Isis Unveiled (1877) and The Secret Doctrine (1888) as patristic precedent for reincarnation, the pre-existence of souls, and esoteric Christianity. His condemnation at Constantinople II (553) is, for her, evidence the Church suppressed the original Gnosis.
source_citation: Blavatsky 'Isis Unveiled' 1877 vol.II ch.5; 'Secret Doctrine' 1888 vol.II

note: Mead's Alexandrian-Gnostic translation programme (Pistis Sophia 1896, Fragments of a Faith Forgotten 1900) reconstructs the milieu Origen taught in; he reads Origen as the bridge between Christian patristics and the Hellenistic mysteries.
